what_it_does: Vitest test suite for ../src/show.js that exercises formatConversationAsMarkdown and formatConversationAsHTML using a fixture JSONL file and three handcrafted Codex JSONL helpers, asserting rendered output contains expected metadata, roles, tool calls, tool results, timestamps, token usage, message ordering, local shell outputs, and HTML escaping.
observations: Uses inline factory functions codexJsonl, codexJsonlWithRawHtml, and codexJsonlWithLocalShellOutput to build JSONL inputs.
Tests both Markdown and HTML renderers with shared fixture tiny-conversation.jsonl.
Expects assistant messages to be labeled Agent in the main thread.
Covers Codex-specific events: session_meta, turn_context, function_call, function_call_output, local_shell_call, local_shell_call_output.
Includes a security-oriented test that raw HTML in messages and tool results is escaped in HTML output.
Asserts token usage appears in compact inline format such as in: X / out: Y.
The sidechain test is only a structural placeholder.
standouts: Explicit HTML escaping test for injected script tags and literal markup in Codex messages and tool I/O.
Coverage of local_shell_call/local_shell_call_output distinct from generic function calls.
Codex rollout JSONL scenario used to validate harness, model, and tool rendering.
conventions: Helper functions return stringified JSON arrays joined by newlines to form JSONL.
Tests use substring presence checks rather than snapshots.
Fixtures are loaded via import.meta.dirname and fs.readFileSync.
concerns: The sidechain test only asserts the markdown is truthy, providing no real behavioral coverage.
Some assertions depend on the unseen contents of tiny-conversation.jsonl, making intent opaque from this file alone.
Date format assertion allows two different formats, which may mask renderer changes.
notes: Vitest spec for conversation rendering that mixes fixture-based regression checks with handcrafted Codex JSONL scenarios covering HTML escaping and local shell outputs.
questions: What exact records and ordering are in tiny-conversation.jsonl?
How does show.js implement HTML escaping and Markdown formatting details?
What is the intended sidechain rendering behavior beyond the placeholder assertion?
Are token usage fields guaranteed to be present in all JSONL inputs?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to test conversation formatting; it largely does so, but sidechain behavior is only placeholder-tested.
intention_signals: To ensure the recollection show command accurately and safely renders conversation logs, including Codex-specific events, tool inputs/outputs, metadata, and local shell activity, in both Markdown and HTML.
novelty: Explicit coverage of raw HTML injection in Codex conversation rendering and of local_shell_call/local_shell_call_output as a separate tool category.
contribution: A behavioral contract for the show formatters, capturing expected Markdown and HTML output for fixtures, Codex JSONL, local shell outputs, and HTML-escaped content, which a fused system would keep as test requirements.
summary_for_embedding: Vitest test file for ../src/show.js verifying Markdown and HTML conversation formatting using fixtures and handcrafted Codex JSONL scenarios covering metadata, tool calls, local shell outputs, hierarchy, token usage, and HTML escaping.
intention_for_embedding: To define and guard the expected rendering behavior of the recollection show command across Markdown and HTML outputs.

symbols.does: Returns a JSONL string representing a minimal Codex rollout with session metadata, turn context, a user message, an exec_command tool call, its output, and an assistant reply.
symbols.description: Builds a five-line JSONL fixture used to validate that the show formatters render Codex harness labels, model info, user prompts, tool invocations, tool results, and assistant responses.

symbols.does: Returns a JSONL string containing raw <script> and <b> markup inside user messages, tool arguments, tool outputs, and assistant messages.
symbols.description: Provides an input scenario specifically designed to test that the HTML renderer escapes literal HTML tags rather than embedding them, preventing injection and preserving literal markup as encoded text.

symbols.does: Returns a JSONL string with a local_shell_call and its local_shell_call_output.
symbols.description: Creates a fixture that exercises rendering of local shell command invocations and their exit-code-plus-output results, distinct from generic function_call tool results.
